tweets.title: Ladakh Border Villagers Raise Grazing Access Issue with Parliamentary Panel Residents of villages along the Line of Actual Control (LAC) in eastern Ladakh have told a Parliamentary Standing Committee that they have lost access to traditional grazing grounds since the 2020 India–China border standoff. The delegation said nomadic pastoralists are no longer permitted to access grazing areas, affecting their livelihoods, and urged the government to allow controlled access in coordination with security agencies. They argued that the continued presence of border communities strengthens national security, describing locals as India's "second line of defence" in frontier regions. The delegation also sought: Recognition of border communities' contributions in national war memorials Better tourism support and relaxation of certain restrictions Upgradation of the strategically important Phobrang–Tsogtsallu road for improved defence logistics and civilian connectivity. The Parliamentary Committee has assured the delegation that it will examine their concerns.
tweets.content:
Ladakh Border Villagers Raise Grazing Access Issue with Parliamentary Panel

Residents of villages along the Line of Actual Control (LAC) in eastern Ladakh have told a Parliamentary Standing Committee that they have lost access to traditional grazing grounds since the 2020 India–China border standoff.

The delegation said nomadic pastoralists are no longer permitted to access grazing areas, affecting their livelihoods, and urged the government to allow controlled access in coordination with security agencies.

They argued that the continued presence of border communities strengthens national security, describing locals as India's "second line of defence" in frontier regions.

The delegation also sought:

Recognition of border communities' contributions in national war memorials

Better tourism support and relaxation of certain restrictions

Upgradation of the strategically important Phobrang–Tsogtsallu road for improved defence logistics and civilian connectivity.

The Parliamentary Committee has assured the delegation that it will examine their concerns.

tweets.title: In recent times, several mid and senior level scientists have left ISRO, and the response now appears to be tighter restrictions on those seeking to resign. Retention cannot be built on restrictions. If you want to keep talented scientists, give them meaningful opportunities, greater autonomy, challenging programmes, and clear career prospects. Otherwise, let them move on. The same principle applies across our strategic R&D ecosystem DRDO, HAL, NAL, and others. India has an exceptional pool of scientific talent, but too often its potential is constrained by mediocre decision-making. Technology is sovereignty. The sooner we recognise that, the better.

tweets.title: NPCIL Downplays Kudankulam Data Breach Concerns India's Nuclear Power Corporation of India Limited (NPCIL) says the reported data breach at the Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant does not affect nuclear safety or reactor operations. According to NPCIL, the leaked data relates to common service and balance-of-plant systems, not the reactor's core or nuclear safety systems. The reported breach involves files allegedly posted by a ransomware group on the dark web. While some documents reportedly include blueprints and supplier details, their authenticity has not been independently verified. This is the second reported cyber incident involving Kudankulam since 2019, though officials maintain that critical nuclear systems remain secure.
tweets.content:
NPCIL Downplays Kudankulam Data Breach Concerns

India's Nuclear Power Corporation of India Limited (NPCIL) says the reported data breach at the Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant does not affect nuclear safety or reactor operations.

According to NPCIL, the leaked data relates to common service and balance-of-plant systems, not the reactor's core or nuclear safety systems.

The reported breach involves files allegedly posted by a ransomware group on the dark web. While some documents reportedly include blueprints and supplier details, their authenticity has not been independently verified.

This is the second reported cyber incident involving Kudankulam since 2019, though officials maintain that critical nuclear systems remain secure.

want to gently circulate indiabuild.wiki - inspired by bomwiki.com

it's a kind of tech map and sovereignty roadmap.

1. idea is to build a catalog of 10,000 products/foundational capabilities India must build. Could range from EDA software to polysilica to naval propulsion systems to port cranes.

2. Will map out current builders, where they're stuck and also add a policy layer on top of this (what is needed to make something happen for ex.).

3. Will also have crowd sourced (including from builders, academics, enthusiasts) inputs leading to our AI engine fleshing out the details.

4. one idea is - once you map out the tech tree - could we identify adjacent players for certain products? for ex: can Bull (CBE based backhoe brand) make port cranes if given support?

4. everything is a little fragile and slightly in bad aesthetic now - it's a side (but important) project largely made this past weekend.

5. it will take a month or so to properly stabilise and have more meaningful data but in the meanwhile I hope you get a sense of what we're trying to build.

6. companies/start-ups and individual builders will also be able to plug in and push their needs, views and accomplishments through an agent very soon.

Dear Mr @MirwaizKashmir,

Words matter.

It's not the "other side of the LoC." It's "Pakistan Occupied Jammu & Kashmir".

Facts don't change with terminology.

Mirwaiz Umar Farooq (@MirwaizKashmir)

Deeply saddened and pained by reports of loss of life of civilians and police personnel in the continuing unrest on the other side of LOC especially in Rawalakot and Poonch. My thoughts and prayers are with the bereaved families and all those affected by these tragic events.

I am also concerned by the ongoing discussions regarding representation and the constitutional and political status of the large number of Kashmiri State Subjects residing in Pakistan. Questions touching upon identity, representation and political rights require sensitivity, dialogue and broad public confidence.

The legitimacy and strength of any system of governance rests ultimately upon the consent, trust and confidence of the people it serves.Our history and our shared social,cultural and family ties across the Line of Control remind us that happenings in one part of Jammu and Kashmir are deeply felt in another, and among our diaspora communities
around the world.

My family has a longstanding historical associations with the social, educational, religious and political development of communities across Jammu and Kashmir, including those on the other side of LOC .

As I continue to hear expressions of anguish and distress from people across the LOC reaching out regarding the unrest, I urge the Govt of Pakistan and the local Government there, to prioritise dialogue, restraint and engagement, resolving the differences through consultation and accommodation rather than confrontation, with respect for human rights and the sanctity of human life remaining paramount.

I also appeal to protesting parties to exercise wisdom and responsibility. Both need to work towards restoring peace and public confidence at the earliest.

I pray for lasting peace and stability for all people of Jammu and Kashmir on both sides of the LOC and its resolution.

tweets.title: US Senate’s New Russia Sanctions Bill. What It Means for India A bipartisan Senate bill unveiled Tuesday (framed as a tribute to the late Sen. Lindsey Graham) proposes tariffs of up to 100% on the top 5 buyers of Russian oil/gas: China, India, Slovakia, Hungary, Azerbaijan. ✅ White House-backed, Could reach a vote before August per Sen. Blumenthal. Key mechanics: ✅ Exact tariff rate (0-100%) to be set by USTR, calibrated to “discourage” continued Russian energy purchases ✅ Exemption for countries importing less than 15% of Russia’s total gas exports AND actively reducing that share ✅ Also hits Russia’s shadow fleet, energy projects, financial institutions Context matters here. This is a softened version of the 2025 Sanctioning Russia Act, which proposed up to 500% tariffs and never got a vote, lacked Trump’s support then. This version has WH backing. That’s the real shift. India’s Russian crude exposure, recent trend: ✅ June 2026: imports hit a record ~2.35 mbpd (full month), touching 2.6 mbpd on some days : highest ever ✅ Russian share of India’s crude basket: as much as 53.5% in June ✅ Preceded by a dip : share fell below 25% between Dec’25-Feb’26 amid sanctions/payment friction, inflows down to ~1.0 mbpd Why the June spike: a US sanctions waiver issued in March (has impact or not need to be studied) after Hormuz tensions pushed crude past $100/bbl, let Indian refiners resume Russian purchases at scale. India remains the world’s second-largest buyer of Russian hydrocarbons. India-US trade exposure (FY26, Apr-Nov’25): ✅ Bilateral trade: $94.39B (record for the period) ✅ India’s exports to US: $58.99B ✅ Trade surplus with US: $23.59B US is India’s #1 export destination.
tweets.content:
US Senate’s New Russia Sanctions Bill.
What It Means for India

A bipartisan Senate bill unveiled Tuesday (framed as a tribute to the late Sen. Lindsey Graham) proposes tariffs of up to 100% on the top 5 buyers of Russian oil/gas: China, India, Slovakia, Hungary, Azerbaijan.
✅ White House-backed, Could reach a vote before August per Sen. Blumenthal.

Key mechanics:
✅ Exact tariff rate (0-100%) to be set by USTR, calibrated to “discourage” continued Russian energy purchases
✅ Exemption for countries importing less than 15% of Russia’s total gas exports AND actively reducing that share
✅ Also hits Russia’s shadow fleet, energy projects, financial institutions

Context matters here. This is a softened version of the 2025 Sanctioning Russia Act, which proposed up to 500% tariffs and never got a vote, lacked Trump’s support then. This version has WH backing. That’s the real shift.

India’s Russian crude exposure, recent trend:
✅ June 2026: imports hit a record ~2.35 mbpd (full month), touching 2.6 mbpd on some days : highest ever
✅ Russian share of India’s crude basket: as much as 53.5% in June
✅ Preceded by a dip : share fell below 25% between Dec’25-Feb’26 amid sanctions/payment friction, inflows down to ~1.0 mbpd

Why the June spike: a US sanctions waiver issued in March (has impact or not need to be studied) after Hormuz tensions pushed crude past $100/bbl, let Indian refiners resume Russian purchases at scale. India remains the world’s second-largest buyer of Russian hydrocarbons.

India-US trade exposure (FY26, Apr-Nov’25):
✅ Bilateral trade: $94.39B (record for the period)
✅ India’s exports to US: $58.99B
✅ Trade surplus with US: $23.59B
US is India’s #1 export destination.

tweets.title: Nominal GDP is a flawed yardstick, sure. But then why is it the ONLY number we flex on the global stage, and never GDP per capita? Same logic applies to defence spending. India’s defence budget is ~30-35% larger than France’s in nominal USD terms ($86- 87B vs ~$65B). Yet we’re the ones showing up with a katora for 114 more Rafales, while running our own AMCA and TEDBF programmes on a shoestring. And spare me the “LCA MK2/Mk1A are delayed” excuse. MMRCA existed well before Mk2 was even a roadmap item.
